-- $Id: testes/db.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

-- testing debug library

local debug = require "debug"

local function dostring(s) return assert(load(s))() end

print"testing debug library and debug information"

do
local a=1
end

assert(not debug.gethook())

local testline = 19         -- line where 'test' is defined
local function test (s, l, p)     -- this must be line 19
  collectgarbage()   -- avoid gc during trace
  local function f (event, line)
    assert(event == 'line')
    local l = table.remove(l, 1)
    if p then print(l, line) end
    assert(l == line, "wrong trace!!")
  end
  debug.sethook(f,"l"); load(s)(); debug.sethook()
  assert(#l == 0)
end


do
  assert(not pcall(debug.getinfo, print, "X"))   -- invalid option
  assert(not pcall(debug.getinfo, 0, ">"))   -- invalid option
  assert(not debug.getinfo(1000))   -- out of range level
  assert(not debug.getinfo(-1))     -- out of range level
  local a = debug.getinfo(print)
  assert(a.what == "C" and a.short_src == "[C]")
  a = debug.getinfo(print, "L")
  assert(a.activelines == nil)
  local b = debug.getinfo(test, "SfL")
  assert(b.name == nil and b.what == "Lua" and b.linedefined == testline and
         b.lastlinedefined == b.linedefined + 10 and
         b.func == test and not string.find(b.short_src, "%["))
  assert(b.activelines[b.linedefined + 1] and
         b.activelines[b.lastlinedefined])
  assert(not b.activelines[b.linedefined] and
         not b.activelines[b.lastlinedefined + 1])
end


--  bug in 5.4.4-5.4.6: activelines in vararg functions
--  without debug information
do
  local func = load(string.dump(load("print(10)"), true))
  local actl = debug.getinfo(func, "L").activelines
  assert(#actl == 0)   -- no line info
end

```c
int oldpc = (L->oldpc < p->sizecode) ? L->oldpc : 0;

    changedline(p, oldpc, npci)) {  /* or when enter new line */
  int newline = luaG_getfuncline(p, npci);
  luaD_hook(L, LUA_HOOKLINE, newline, 0, 0);  /* call line hook */


}
L->oldpc = npci;  /* 'pc' of last call to line hook */
```

The logic fires the line hook when we jump backward (detecting a loop) or cross into a new line, then updates the global `oldpc` to the current position. The critical issue is that `L->oldpc` is a global state variable shared across all function frames—when function A calls function B, `oldpc` tracks B's execution, and when B returns, A resumes with B's stale `oldpc` value still in place.

When the hook executes inside `trace_exec`, it recursively calls itself for the hook function's instructions, which modifies `oldpc`. After the hook returns, we reset `oldpc` back to the current instruction position for the executing function. However, if the hook never fires due to the `ci_lua_closure` guard, `oldpc` doesn't get reset, which could cause issues on the next iteration.

The `load` function creates a new closure, and when it's called, the VM enters `execute` with a new call info pointing to that closure. end

1295	///
1296	/// Mirrors C's `jumponcond` from `lcode.c`. The `OP_NOT` peephole that C
1297	/// applies for `VRELOC` operands is intentionally skipped for the Phase-A
1298	/// bootstrap; correctness is unaffected and the optimisation can land with
1299	/// the codegen reconciliation pass.
1300	fn cg_jump_on_cond(
1301	    fs: &mut FuncState,
1302	    line: i32,
1303	    e: &mut ExprDesc,
1304	    cond: u8,
1305	) -> Result<i32, LuaError> {
1306	    let reg = cg_exp_to_any_reg(fs, line, e)?;
1307	    cg_free_exp(fs, e);
1308	    let test = lua_code::opcodes::Instruction::abck(
1309	        lua_code::opcodes::OpCode::TestSet,
1310	        lua_code::opcodes::NO_REG,
1311	        reg as u32,
1312	        0,
1313	        cond as u32,
1314	    );
1315	    emit_inst(fs, line, test);
1316	    Ok(cg_jump(fs, line))
1317	}
1318	
1319	/// First half of `luaK_posfix`: pre-process the left operand `v` of a binary
1320	/// operator before the right operand is parsed. Mirrors C's `luaK_infix`
1321	/// from `lcode.c`. The codegen reconciliation has not yet routed parser
1322	/// calls through `lua_code::infix`, so this lives in the parser file
1323	/// alongside the other `cg_*` helpers.
1324	///
1325	/// For `And`/`Or` the operand is converted into a short-circuit form (jump
1326	/// list closed via `cg_go_if_true` / `cg_go_if_false`). For `Concat` it is
1327	/// pushed onto the next register. Other arithmetic, bitwise, and comparison
1328	/// operators rely on `cg_posfix_fold` to discharge their operands after the
1329	/// right-hand side is known, so `cg_infix` only calls `cg_discharge_vars`
1330	/// for them.
1331	fn cg_infix(
1332	    fs: &mut FuncState,
1333	    op: BinOpr,
1334	    v: &mut ExprDesc,
1335	    line: i32,
1336	) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
1337	    match op {
1338	        BinOpr::And => cg_go_if_true(fs, line, v),
1339	        BinOpr::Or => cg_go_if_false(fs, line, v),
1340	        BinOpr::Concat => cg_exp_to_next_reg(fs, line, v),
1341	        BinOpr::Add | BinOpr::Sub | BinOpr::Mul | BinOpr::Div | BinOpr::IDiv
1342	        | BinOpr::Mod | BinOpr::Pow
1343	        | BinOpr::BAnd | BinOpr::BOr | BinOpr::BXor
1344	        | BinOpr::Shl | BinOpr::Shr
1345	        | BinOpr::Eq | BinOpr::Ne
1346	        | BinOpr::Lt | BinOpr::Le | BinOpr::Gt | BinOpr::Ge => {
1347	            if matches!(v.k, ExprKind::KInt | ExprKind::KFlt)
1348	                && v.t == NO_JUMP && v.f == NO_JUMP
1349	            {
1350	                cg_discharge_vars(fs, line, v)
1351	            } else {
1352	                cg_exp_to_any_reg(fs, line, v).map(|_| ())
1353	            }
1354	        }

1. Primary Request and Intent:

The user is running as the `test-fixer` role. The task is to fix the Rust Lua port so the failing official Lua test `db.lua` passes — specifically the assertion `#l == 0` at `db.lua:28`. The packet identified three failing fixtures (`db`, `gc`, `gengc`) and directed investigation to `crates/lua-vm/src/debug.rs:1900-1960` and a "recently-added C-frame guard." The packet asked to:

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- **Lua line hooks**: `debug.sethook(f, 'l')` fires `f(event, line)` on each new source line executed
- **`trace_exec` / `luaG_traceexec`**: The C function called before each VM instruction to dispatch line/count hooks. In Rust: `crates/lua-vm/src/debug.rs::trace_exec`
- **`lastline` vs `linenumber`**: In C Lua's `lcode.c`, `luaK_code` always uses `fs->ls->lastline` (line of last consumed token) for instruction line info — NOT `linenumber` (current scanner position). `lastline` is set to `linenumber` at the START of `luaX_next` before reading the next token
- **`changed_line`**: Detects if line changed between two instruction PCs in a proto's lineinfo
- **`oldpc`**: Global state variable tracking the last PC traced for line hooks. Shared across all frames — can become stale when crossing function boundaries
- **C-frame guard** (lines 1920-1922 of debug.rs): `if state.ci_lua_closure(ci_idx).is_none() { return Ok(1); }` — guard added to prevent trace_exec from operating on non-Lua frames
- **`state.ci` corruption**: When a Lua hook function throws an error that is swallowed, `state.ci` is left pointing to an incorrect CI frame because `poscall` (which restores `state.ci`) is not called on error paths
- **Hook error swallowing**: In `debug_lib.rs`, `let _ = hookf(st, ar.event, ar.currentline)` discards all errors from the Lua hook function
- **Instruction line number assignment**: How the Rust Lua compiler assigns source line numbers to bytecode instructions
- **Active lines**: The set of source lines that have at least one bytecode instruction attributed to them

3. Files and Code Sections:

- **`harness/evidence/runs/20260522T033259Z-9d11e4c-runner-baseline-gc.json`**:
  - Evidence blob showing gc.lua failing; db.lua failure shown at `db.combined.lua:51` = db.lua:28

  - The failing assertion is at line 28: `assert(#l == 0)`
  - First call at line 124: `test([[if\nmath.sin(1)\nthen\n  a=1\nelse\n  a=2\nend\n]], {2,3,4,7})`
  - Third call at line 145: `test([[--\nif nil then\n  a=1\nelse\n  a=2\nend\n]], {2,5,6})` ← THIS IS THE FAILING CALL

- **`crates/lua-vm/src/debug.rs` (lines 1878-1975)**:
  - `trace_exec()` function — dispatches line/count hooks per VM instruction
  - The "C-frame guard" at lines 1920-1922:
    ```rust
    if state.ci_lua_closure(ci_idx).is_none() {
        return Ok(1);
    }
    ```
  - Line events check at 1940-1960 using `changed_line` and `get_func_line`
  - The packet pointed here but the actual bug is elsewhere

- **`crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs` (line 2445)**:
  - `pub fn trace_exec(&mut self, _idx: CallInfoIdx, pc: u32)` — the `_idx` parameter is ignored!
    ```rust

        Ok(crate::debug::trace_exec(self, pc)? != 0)
    }
    ```

- **`crates/lua-stdlib/src/debug_lib.rs` (lines 636-661, 707-781)**:
  - `hookf()` at line 636: calls the Lua hook function, returns `Result<(), LuaError>`
  - `set_hook()` at line 711: wraps hookf in a closure that DISCARDS errors:
    ```rust
    Some(Box::new(|st, ar| {
        let _ = hookf(st, ar.event, ar.currentline);  // BUG: errors silently discarded
    }))
    ```

- **`crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs` (lines 397-476)**:
  - `hook()` function — the C `luaD_hook` equivalent
  - Does NOT restore `state.ci = ci_idx` after `h(state, &ar)` returns
  - Missing: `state.ci = ci_idx;` before `Ok(())`

- **`crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs`** — **THE ROOT CAUSE FILE**:
  - **Line 3778** in `restassign` (the critical fix):
    ```rust

    let line = ls.linenumber;  // should be ls.lastline
    ```
    After `explist()` parses `2` in `a=2`, the next token is `end` on line 6. So `ls.linenumber=6, ls.lastline=5`. Using `linenumber` puts SETTABUP at line 6; using `lastline` puts it at line 5 (correct).
  
  - **Line 3785** in `restassign` (multi-assign path):
    ```rust

    ```

    ```rust
    let line = ls.linenumber;  // should be ls.lastline (TEST instruction should be at 'then' line)
    cg_go_if_true(ls.fs.as_mut().unwrap(), line, &mut v)?;
    ```
  
  - **Line 4138** in `test_then_block`:
    ```rust

    let line = ls.linenumber;  // should be ls.lastline (JMP should be at last then-block line)
    let j = cg_jump(ls.fs.as_mut().unwrap(), line);
    ```
  
  - **Line 4365** in `retstat`:
    ```rust

    cg_emit_return(ls.fs.as_mut().unwrap(), line, first, nret);
    ```

- **`reference/lua-c/llex.c` (line 565)**:
  - C Lua's `luaX_next` sets `ls->lastline = ls->linenumber` BEFORE reading the next token
  - This means `lastline` = line of the PREVIOUS (just-consumed) token

- **`reference/lua-c/lcode.c` (line 382)**:
  - C Lua's `luaK_code` uses `fs->ls->lastline` (not `linenumber`) for ALL instruction line info:

4. Errors and Fixes:

- **Packet hypothesis was wrong**: The packet claimed the bug was in `debug.rs`'s "C-frame guard" being "too lax." Through investigation, the actual bug was found in the Lua compiler (`lua-parse/src/lib.rs`). The C-frame guard is not the root cause.

- **Hook error swallowing masks mismatches**: The `let _ = hookf(...)` in `debug_lib.rs` discards all errors from Lua hook functions. This means when the hook's `assert(ll == line)` fails (wrong line number), the error is silently dropped and test execution continues. This makes tests appear to pass even with wrong line numbers — as long as the NUMBER of fires matches the number of expected items. Test 3 fails precisely because the count doesn't match (2 fires vs. 3 expected).

- **`state.ci` corruption after hook error**: When a Lua hook function errors and the error is swallowed, `state.ci` is left pointing to the hook function's CI (or its callees). This secondary bug exists but doesn't directly cause test 3 to fail in the way observed.

5. Problem Solving:

**Root cause traced through multiple layers:**

1. `db.lua:28` asserts `#l == 0` — fails for the 3rd `test()` call
2. Test 3 uses chunk `[[--\nif nil then\n  a=1\nelse\n  a=2\nend\n]]` with expected `{2,5,6}`
3. Our impl fires only 2 hook events (lines 3, 6); expected 3 fires (lines 2, 5, 6)
4. Missing fire: `a=2` instruction at line 5 doesn't generate an event because it's merged at line 6 (same as RETURN)
5. Root cause: in `restassign` (lua-parse/src/lib.rs line 3778), `ls.linenumber` is used after `explist()` — this gives the line of the NEXT token (`end` = line 6) instead of `ls.lastline` (line of `2` = line 5)
6. C Lua uses `fs->ls->lastline` everywhere in code generation (via `luaK_code`), never `linenumber`

**Disassembly comparison confirmed the bug:**
- C Lua `luac -l` shows TEST at `[3]`, SETTABUP a=2 at `[5]`, RETURN at `[6]` (via `lastline`)
- Our impl shows active lines `{3, 4, 6}` — TEST at 3 (should be 2), no line 5, SETTABUP+RETURN merged at 6

**Impact analysis of the fix:**
- Test 3: 2 fires → 3 fires → PASSES (a=2 at line 5 now generates separate event)
- Test 1: fires stay at 4 (else branch not executed) → PASSES
- Test 4: a=1 moves from line 2→1, a=a+1 moves from line 4→3 → fires become `{1,3,4,3,4}` exactly matching expected `{1,3,4,3,4}` (cleaner pass)
- Test 5: return moves from line 3→2, matches expected `{2}` (cleaner pass)
- Tests 2, 6: counts may change slightly but both currently pass with extras (need verification)

8. Current Work:

Immediately before this summary request, the investigation had fully traced the bug to `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs`. No edits have been made yet. The analysis showed:

**The bug** is at line 3778 in the `restassign` function:
```rust
// lua-parse/src/lib.rs, inside restassign(), the else branch for nexps == nvars:
let nexps = explist(ls, state, &mut e)?;
if nexps != nvars {
    adjust_assign(ls, state, nvars, nexps, &mut e)?;
} else {
    let line = ls.linenumber;  // ← BUG: after explist(), linenumber is NEXT token's line
    let fs = ls.fs.as_mut().unwrap();
    cg_set_one_ret(fs, &mut e);
    cg_storevar(fs, line, &lh.v, &mut e)?;  // emits SETTABUP at wrong line
    return Ok(());
}
```

For the chunk `if nil then a=1 else a=2 end`, `a=2` is on line 5. After `explist()` parses `2`, the next token is `end` on line 6. So `ls.linenumber=6` but `ls.lastline=5`. Using `linenumber` places the SETTABUP instruction at line 6 (merged with RETURN), giving only 2 line events. Using `lastline` places SETTABUP at line 5, giving 3 line events matching the expected `{2,5,6}`.

**Confirmed empirically**: Running test 3 currently produces `fires=2 fires_at=3,6` with `remaining=1`, causing `assert(#l == 0)` to fail.

The specific change needed:
```rust

// AFTER:

```

This is the direct fix consistent with C Lua's use of `fs->ls->lastline` in `luaK_code`.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and runs the combined
# file through the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src_file" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src_file" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src_file" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

rc=0
run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || rc=$?

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if [ "$rc" = "0" ] \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|pcall_k failed" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
elif grep -qE "pcall_k failed" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-error"
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented|pcall_k failed|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1
